body:
### Problem

Neovim seems to already track terminal alternate-screen state internally, but does not expose it to Lua/plugins.

```c
// https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/src/nvim/terminal.c#L174
bool in_altscreen;
```

Use case: terminal plugins need to know when an interactive TUI is active (nvim, less, fzf, htop, etc.) to avoid overriding terminal input mappings such as <Esc>.

### Expected behavior

Allow access as buffer property: `b:term_in_altscreen`

Or maybe events for enter and leave:
TermAltScreenEnter
TermAltScreenLeave

comment_data.body:
by "state" you mean a flag, not "the entire screen state"?

> Allow access as buffer property: `b:term_in_altscreen`

could be added to `nvim_get_chan_info()` (+ `bracketedpaste`, etc.). https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35414#issuecomment-3221546810

> Or maybe events for enter and leave: TermAltScreenEnter, TermAltScreenLeave

Instead of adding new events for every terminal mode-change, how about a single `TermProperty{kind:string}` event:

## Proposal: introduce `TermProperty`, extend `TermRequest`

### Problem

Nvim tracks terminal state internally (e.g. `in_altscreen` in `terminal.c`) but doesn't expose it.

> [!NOTE]
> I wondered if we could overload `TermRequest` for CSI seqs, but doesn't fit because (1) semantic mismatch (`TermRequest` is for "requests", whereas CSI is usually a rendering/state command); and (2) CSI is already parsed (`leader`, `args[]`, …), we would have to re-serialize it back to a raw `\x1b[…` string (lossy/awkward) to fit the `TermRequest` shape.

### Proposal

(Note: This proposal is compatible with Nvim's eventual migration from libvterm to libghostty.)

| Event          | Triggers on                       | Payload key | Grows by      |
|----------------|-----------------------------------|-------------|---------------|
| `TermProperty` | recognized modes/state            | `prop`      | new prop name |
| `TermRequest`  | unrecognized OSC/DCS/APC/CSI seqs | `kind`      | new kind      |


- `TermRequest` = unrecognized sequences.
    - a new recognized feature is a new `prop` value; a new raw sequence class is a new `kind`.
- `TermProperty` = a (recognized) "state" or "mode" change. Has a "shape" instead of just raw bytes.

### 1. `TermProperty` (new): recognized modes/state

*Deferred* event (`aucmd_defer`). Emitted from `term_settermprop`, one line per `VTERM_PROP_*` case. 

```lua
-- autocmd `data`:
{
  prop  = "alt_screen",   -- friendly Nvim name from the `term_settermprop` switch:
                          -- (cursor_visible, cursor_shape, title, icon_name, mouse, focus_report …)
  value = true            -- typed per prop: bool | string | number | enum-string
}
```

Example:

```lua
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("TermProperty", {
  callback = function(ev)
    if ev.data.prop == "alt_screen" then
      vim.b[ev.buf].term_in_altscreen = ev.data.value
    end
  end,
})
```

### 2. (low priority) Extend `TermRequest` for unrecognized sequences (OSC/DCS/APC + CSI)

Wire `on_csi` into `vterm_fallbacks.csi` (the `default:` case in `vterm/state.c`):

- only *unrecognized* CSI reaches it (recognized CSI such as SGR/cursor-moves/alt-screen never does, so volume stays bounded).
    - TODO: how noisy would this be?
- make `terminator` optional
- add new optional fields for CSI kind:
  ```lua
  -- autocmd `data`:
  { kind       = "csi",            -- NEW: "osc" | "dcs" | "apc" | "csi"
    sequence   = "\027[?1049h",    -- raw bytes, uniform across all kinds
    cursor     = { row, col },
    terminator = nil,              -- now OPTIONAL: BEL/ST for string seqs; absent for CSI
  
    -- OPTIONAL, present when kind="csi" (already parsed in the fallback):
    leader     = "?",
    args       = { 1049 },
    intermed   = "",
    command    = "h" }
  ```
- `v:termrequest` continues to hold the raw `sequence`.
- The block-and-reply plumbing (`pending.send`) is unchanged — idle for most CSI, still available for genuine queries.
- `kind` lets consumers stop sniffing leading escape bytes (`\027]` / `\027P` / `\027_` / `\027[`).

milestone.description: Low priority items. We plan to work on this but don't have a target date.
milestone.title: backlog

title: `TermProperty` event (was: "Expose terminal alternate-screen state")

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
